芋.name: Tuber
芋.meaning: Edible tuber such as potato, sweet potato, yam, or taro.
芋.reviewNote: Checked imo and u readings, edible tuber glosses, and 艹 + 于 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

英.name: English
英.reviewNote: Checked ei and hanabusa readings, Britain, English, and outstanding glosses, and 艹 + 央 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

花.name: Flower
花.meaning: Flower; a basic kanji for blossoms and flowers.
花.reviewNote: Checked hana and ka readings, flower gloss, and 艹 + 化 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

茄.name: Eggplant

荷.name: Load
荷.meaning: Load, cargo, or luggage; also to shoulder or carry.
荷.reviewNote: Checked ni, ninau, and ka readings, load, cargo, and luggage glosses, and 艹 + 何 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

菓.name: Confectionery
菓.meaning: Confectionery or fruit; the kanji used in words such as 菓子.
菓.reviewNote: Checked kashi, kudamono, and ka readings, confection or fruit usage, and 艹 + 果 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芽.name: Bud
芽.meaning: Bud, sprout, or shoot; also the germ or beginning of an idea.

苦.name: Bitter
苦.meaning: Bitter; also pain, suffering, or hardship.
苦.reviewNote: Checked nigai, kurushii, and ku readings, bitter and suffering glosses, and 艹 + 古 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

若.name: Young
若.meaning: Young; also used for conditional meanings such as if.
若.reviewNote: Checked wakai and jaku readings, young and if glosses, and 艹 + 右 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

苗.name: Seedling

芳.name: Fragrant
芳.meaning: Fragrant or sweet-smelling; also good reputation or satisfactory.
芳.reviewNote: Checked kanbashii and ho readings, fragrant, good reputation, and satisfactory glosses, and 艹 + 方 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.
